title: 8 Herbal Essential Oils For Gum Pain
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils can be a great way to relieve gum pain. You see, certain herbs have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help reduce swelling and fight off infections in the mouth. When you use these herbs in the form of essential oils, they can be easily absorbed through the skin or inhaled, making them a convenient and effective treatment for gum pain.  For example, Eucalyptus globulus has decongestant properties that help reduce swelling in the gums, while Mentha x piperita, or peppermint, has a cooling effect that can numb the pain. Salvia officinalis, or sage, has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gums. When you use these essential oils, you can apply them topically to your gums or inhale them through steam inhalation to get relief from pain and discomfort.  Herbal teas made from these same herbs can also provide relief from gum pain. When you drink these teas, the compounds are absorbed into your bloodstream and can help reduce inflammation and fight off infections. Drinking herbal teas can also help keep your mouth moist and reduce the risk of further irritation. This can be especially helpful if you have a condition like dry mouth, which can exacerbate gum pain. By incorporating herbal essential oils and teas into your daily routine, you can find relief from gum pain and promote overall oral health.
intro_image_alt: herbal essential oils for gum pain

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils contains a compound called eugenol, which has a numbing effect on the gums, helping to reduce pain. It also contains cineole, a natural anti-inflammatory that reduces swelling and redness in the gums. The anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties of eucalyptus globulus essential oils help to fight off infections and inflammation in the gums, promoting healing and reducing pain. Additionally, the oil's analgesic properties help to block pain signals to the brain, providing relief from gum pain. By applying eucalyptus globulus essential oils to the affected area, you can take advantage of its pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory properties to alleviate gum pain.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal essential oils for gum pain
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Eucalyptus globulus leaves and 2 cups of water in a large pot.
Heat the water in the pot over low heat, then add the Eucalyptus leaves.
Let the mixture simmer for 2-3 hours. This will help release the essential oils from the leaves.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container. Discard the solids.
Store the Eucalyptus globulus essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid. Use 5-7 drops in a carrier oil or apply topically for gum pain relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils contains a compound called menthol, which is known for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. Menthol helps to numb the affected area, providing temporary relief from gum pain. The essential oil also contains limonene, which has antibacterial properties that can help reduce inflammation and prevent infection. The menthone in Mentha x piperita essential oils has a cooling effect, which can help reduce swelling and ease pain. By applying Mentha x piperita essential oils to the affected area, you can take advantage of its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties to alleviate gum pain.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal essential oils for gum pain
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh peppermint leaves (Mentha x piperita), a clean glass jar, a dark glass bottle, and a cheesecloth.
Wash the peppermint leaves with 1 cup of cool water, and pat them dry with a paper towel.
Fill the glass jar with the peppermint leaves, and pour 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or jojoba oil) over them.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily. Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into the dark glass bottle.
Discard the solids and store the peppermint essential oil in the dark glass bottle. Use 5-7 drops per application to relieve gum pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis essential oils contains rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid, which are powerful antioxidants. These antioxidants help to reduce inflammation in the gum tissue, making it a natural remedy for gum pain caused by inflammation. The anti-inflammatory properties of rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid also help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums. Additionally, Salvia officinalis essential oils contains thymol, a compound that has antibacterial properties, which can help to prevent infections in the gum tissue that can cause pain. By reducing inflammation and preventing infections, Salvia officinalis essential oils can help to alleviate gum pain and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal essential oils for gum pain
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves. Choose leaves with no signs of mold or damage.
Wash the leaves in 1 cup of cold water to remove dirt and bacteria. Dry with a clean towel.
In a blender, combine 1 cup of the dried leaves with 4 cups of water. Blend until the leaves are well mixed.
Transfer the mixture to a saucepan and heat on low for 2 hours. Monitor the temperature (not to exceed 100°F).
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl. Discard the solids. Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils contains compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which are known for their anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help reduce swelling and pain in the gum area. The bioactive constituents in Zingiber officinale, including gingerol and shogaol, inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause pain and inflammation. The warming sensation from ginger essential oils can increase blood flow and reduce pain, making it a potential remedy for gum pain. By reducing inflammation and promoting blood flow, Zingiber officinale essential oils may help alleviate gum pain and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for gum pain
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Zingiber officinale roots, a cup of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil), and a glass jar with a lid.
Wash the Zingiber officinale roots with water, then chop them into small pieces.
Combine the chopped roots and carrier oil in a glass jar. Let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the Zingiber officinale essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid. Use 5-7 drops of the oil for gum pain relief, mixed with a carrier oil for application.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils contains compounds like cineole, limonene, and beta-pinene. These bioactive constituents have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce gum pain and swelling. Cineole, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause gum infections. The anti-inflammatory properties of limonene and beta-pinene help to reduce redness and pain in the gums, making it easier to manage gum pain. The combination of these properties in Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils makes it a natural remedy for gum pain and inflammation.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal essential oils for gum pain
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Melaleuca alternifolia leaves and 1 cup of water in a saucepan.
Boil the water and add the leaves. Let it simmer for 30 minutes.
Let the mixture cool down. Strain the liquid into a bowl using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Add 1 tablespoon of carrier oil (like coconut or jojoba oil) to the liquid. Mix well.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle. Use a few drops to apply topically for gum pain relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils contains a compound called alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums. The oil also contains cichoric acid, a compound that has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help fight off bacteria that can cause gum infections. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea essential oils contains isobutyl amide, a compound that has been found to have analgesic properties, which can help alleviate pain. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of Echinacea purpurea essential oils can help reduce gum pain and inflammation, making it a potential natural remedy for gum problems. The use of Echinacea purpurea essential oils may be particularly beneficial when used in conjunction with other natural remedies for gum health.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal essential oils for gum pain
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves, 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut or jojoba oil), a clean glass jar, a cheesecloth, and a dark glass bottle.
Combine the Echinacea flowers and leaves in the clean glass jar, and cover them with the carrier oil. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through the cheesecloth into the dark glass bottle. Discard the solids and keep the oil.
Filter the oil again through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any remaining solids. Transfer the oil to a clean, dark glass bottle.

Store the Echinacea essential oil in a cool, dark place and use it as needed to relieve gum pain. Use 5-7 drops of the oil in a carrier oil (coconut or jojoba oil) and apply it to the affected area with a cotton swab.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oils contains a compound called curcumin, which is a powerful anti-inflammatory. This helps to reduce swelling and pain in the gums. The oil also contains other bioactive constituents like demethoxycurcumin and bisdemethoxycurcumin, which have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to fight off free radicals that can cause gum pain and inflammation, promoting healthy gum tissue. By reducing inflammation and fighting off free radicals, Curcuma longa essential oils helps to alleviate gum pain and promote overall gum health.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal essential oils for gum pain
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Curcuma longa root, 2 cups of water, and a saucepan.
Wash the Curcuma longa root under running water, then chop it into small pieces.
Place the chopped root in the saucepan and add 2 cups of water. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 2 hours.
After 2 hours, turn off the heat and let the mixture cool. Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl.
Transfer the liquid to a dark glass bottle and store it in the fridge. Use 5-10 drops of the oil as needed to relieve gum pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils contains rosmarinic acid, camphor, and borneol as its active constituents. These compounds have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce gum pain. Rosmarinic acid, in particular, has been shown to have antioxidant properties that can help to reduce swelling and prevent infection in the gums. The antimicrobial properties of borneol and camphor can help to eliminate bacteria that can cause gum pain and inflammation. By reducing inflammation and preventing infection, Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils can help to alleviate gum pain and promote healthy gums.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal essential oils for gum pain
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water in the saucepan over low heat for 10-15 minutes.
Add the Rosmarinus officinalis leaves to the water and continue heating for another 10-15 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a glass bottle. Discard the solids.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent gum pain from forming. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the gums and reduce swelling. These teas can also help calm the mouth and reduce stress, which can contribute to gum pain. This can lead to healthier gums and a reduced risk of pain.
faq_safe: While some herbal teas may help soothe gum pain, consuming them daily can have mixed effects. Some teas, like peppermint and chamomile, are gentle and safe to drink regularly. However, other teas, like those with a high concentration of tannins, like sage or licorice root, may cause stomach issues or interact with medications if consumed excessively.
faq_timeline: For herbal teas to show results in gum pain, it can take anywhere from a few days to a week or two. Some people may notice relief within 24 to 48 hours, while others may take longer. It depends on the individual and the severity of the pain. Regular consumption is usually necessary to feel the benefits.
faq_time: For gum pain, consider drinking herbal tea in the morning or early afternoon. This allows the tea to help with inflammation and discomfort throughout the day. The anti-inflammatory properties in the tea may be more effective when your body is most active, making it easier to manage pain.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for gum pain, especially if they contain active ingredients like ginkgo biloba or St. John's Wort. These ingredients can affect how medications work in the body, leading to changes in pain levels or other side effects. This is because herbal teas can alter the way the body breaks down certain medications.
faq_pregnant: When you're pregnant and have gum pain, some herbal teas might seem like a good option. However, it's essential to be cautious with what you consume. Some herbal teas, like peppermint or chamomile, are generally considered safe. But others, like sage or pennyroyal, might not be suitable due to potential risks to your baby.
faq_breastfeeding: If you're breastfeeding and experiencing gum pain, some herbal teas might be worth trying. Peppermint tea, for example, is often used to ease pain and discomfort. However, it's essential to check the ingredients and ensure they're safe for consumption while nursing. Some herbs, like sage, might not be suitable.
